docs.title: AI Architect · ML Platform Architect · Principal AI Engineer
docs.text:
I design and build production AI platforms, MLOps systems, AI agent applications and machine learning solutions for industrial and enterprise environments.

View projects Architecture Download résumé

docs.title: Areas of expertise
docs.text:
• Enterprise AI

    Production AI systems for industrial and engineering workflows.

  • MLOps Platforms

docs.title: Professional profile
docs.text:
• More than 20 years in software engineering, applied mathematics and AI
  • PhD in Theoretical Physics
  • AI Architect and ML Team Lead
  • Course Director for Machine Learning, MLOps, Reinforcement Learning and Quantitative Finance
  • Experience designing secure enterprise AI platforms

docs.title: From theoretical physics to production AI
docs.text:
I hold a PhD in Theoretical Physics from Lomonosov Moscow State University and spent over 20 years applying mathematics to hard industrial problems: graph optimisation for national pipeline networks, hydraulic modelling, Markov-chain reliability models — long before "data science" became a job title.

Today I work as an AI Architect and ML Team Lead, designing production AI platforms for industrial and enterprise environments: MLOps systems, AI agents for engineering workflows, predictive analytics at national-infrastructure scale.

docs.title: How I work
docs.text:
• Architecture before hype. Every AI subsystem I design starts with the operating constraints: security requirements, data locality, the people who will actually use it.
  • Production is the finish line. A model that isn't deployed, monitored and maintained doesn't exist. I build the full life cycle — from requirements to on-site rollout and operational support.
  • Teams over heroes. I've led multi-disciplinary teams of 20+ specialists and directed four course tracks at OTUS. Explaining complex systems clearly is half of an architect's job.

docs.title: Beyond engineering
docs.text:
• Course Director at OTUS for Machine Learning, MLOps, Reinforcement Learning and Quantitative Finance tracks.
  • Studied physics at Philipps-Universität Marburg, Germany.
  • Experience evaluating the economic side of engineering: risk assessment, feasibility studies, investment effectiveness.

Availability

Open to AI Architect / ML Lead roles — relocation to New Zealand or fully remote.

docs.title: Course Director
docs.text: Track Scope Reinforcement Learning From MDPs and value-based methods to production RL agents MLOps Production deployment of ML models — the full life cycle Machine Learning: Basic & Professional Core ML through advanced practice Quantitative Finance Quant analysis, algorithmic trading

docs.title: Lecture topics
docs.text:
AI architecture · AI agents in production · machine learning · reinforcement learning · time-series analysis · cloud infrastructure · quantitative finance · algorithmic trading · Python OOP.

Why it matters for architecture work

Directing four course tracks means constantly re-explaining complex systems to smart people from scratch — the same skill an architect needs when aligning engineering teams and stakeholders.

docs.title: Industrial AI Assistants
docs.text: 2024 — present · DM-Solutions · AI Architect

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text: Production and industrial engineering workflows are full of expert knowledge locked in documentation, regulations and the heads of senior engineers. The goal: LLM-based assistants that make this knowledge operational — without sending sensitive industrial data to external APIs.

docs.title: My role
docs.text:
• Designed AI assistants for industrial and production-engineering workflows.
  • Architected the AI subsystem for developing predictive models.
  • Designed deployment infrastructure for production operation of models.
  • Led the model development and training teams.
  • Adapted the architecture to security-compliance requirements (FSTEC).

docs.title: Architecture highlights
docs.text:
• Agent orchestration with tool integration: assistants query predictive models, documentation and enterprise systems rather than hallucinating answers.
  • On-premise LLM serving — no external API dependency, full data locality.
  • Role-specific assistants: a production engineer and an ML engineer get different capabilities on the same platform core.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text: LangChain · LangGraph · RAG · PyTorch · Kubernetes · KServe · MLFlow · Apache Airflow

docs.title: Enterprise AI Platform — AtomMind 2.0
docs.text: 2024 · TVEL Rosatom JSC · AI Architect

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text: The nuclear-fuel manufacturer needed a unified platform to manage the full life cycle of ML solutions — from model creation to production operation — usable by production engineers, not only data scientists, and operating in a regulated, security-constrained environment.

docs.title: My role
docs.text:
End-to-end technical architecture:

  • Requirements for the ML life-cycle management system.
  • Platform technical architecture and implementation options.
  • Design of the MLOps stack.
  • Integration of the MLOps subsystem into the overall AtomMind 2.0 platform.
  • Technical implementation design of the MLOps subsystem.

docs.title: Architecture highlights
docs.text:
Two-level AI-assistant design

One assistant tier for production engineers on the shop floor, another for model creation and management — different UX, shared platform core.

  • MLOps subsystem covering training, validation, deployment and monitoring.
  • Designed for national security-compliance requirements (FSTEC) — air-gapped operation, controlled dependencies, auditable pipelines.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text: Kubernetes · KubeFlow · KServe · MLFlow · Apache Airflow · LangChain · LangGraph

docs.title: OCR Platform for Technical Documents
docs.text: 2024 · DM-Solutions · ML Team Lead

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text: Automated recognition of technical text in engineering documentation — domain-specific notation, tables and drawings that general-purpose OCR handles poorly.

docs.title: My role
docs.text:
• Led development and automation of ML pipelines for technical text recognition.
  • Built pipelines for model quality assurance — recognition quality is measured continuously, not assumed.
  • Model testing and preparation for production deployment.

docs.title: Architecture highlights
docs.text:
• Automated end-to-end pipeline: ingestion → preprocessing → recognition → quality scoring.
  • QA pipeline as a first-class component: every model version is validated against a reference corpus before promotion.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text: PyTorch · Transformers · OCR · Apache Airflow · Docker · MLFlow

Stack: Python · Time series · RL · A/B testing · MLFlow · ClickHouse

docs.title: Trading bot — strategy models in production
docs.text:

docs.title: Teaching RL — OTUS
docs.text:
2023 — present · Course Director

Directing the Reinforcement Learning course track: from Markov decision processes and value-based methods to policy gradients and production deployment of RL agents. See Teaching.

Why offline evaluation matters

In trading — as in industrial control — you cannot A/B-test your way out of a bad policy without paying for it. Offline policy evaluation and careful simulation are what make RL deployable in high-stakes environments.
